AAON.headline: Nasdaq 100 Climbs Records, Oil Extends Drop As Iran Weighs Hormuz Deal: Stock Market Today
AAON.description: U.S. tech stocks reached record highs on May 7, 2026, as crude oil tumbled nearly 3% on hopes Iran will accept a U.S. memorandum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Nasdaq 100 gained 0.4% to 28,706 while the S&P 500 rose 0.2% to 7,376. Earnings drove significant stock-specific moves, with major winners including AAON (+36%), Datadog (+30%), and H&R Block (+26%), while notable losers included Planet Fitness (-33%), Insmed (-22%), and Zoetis (-22%).

DDOG.headline: Nasdaq 100 Climbs Records, Oil Extends Drop As Iran Weighs Hormuz Deal: Stock Market Today
DDOG.description: U.S. tech stocks reached record highs on May 7, 2026, as crude oil tumbled nearly 3% on hopes Iran will accept a U.S. memorandum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Nasdaq 100 gained 0.4% to 28,706 while the S&P 500 rose 0.2% to 7,376. Earnings drove significant stock-specific moves, with major winners including AAON (+36%), Datadog (+30%), and H&R Block (+26%), while notable losers included Planet Fitness (-33%), Insmed (-22%), and Zoetis (-22%).

DDOG.headline: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down On May 7?
DDOG.description: The S&P 500 rallied to an all-time high on May 7, 2026, climbing 1.46% to 7,365.12 as investors responded positively to reports of potential U.S.-Iran peace negotiations. Oil prices fell sharply on de-escalation hopes, boosting equities. Polymarket traders are leaning bullish for Thursday's opening, with strong corporate earnings and AI momentum supporting the rally. President Trump cautioned that no agreement has been finalized.

HIMX.headline: Himax Technologies, Inc. Declares Cash Dividend for FY2025
HIMX.description: Himax Technologies (NASDAQ: HIMX) announced a cash dividend of 25.2 cents per ADS (12.6 cents per ordinary share) for fiscal year 2025, with a 100% payout ratio reflecting the company's healthy balance sheet and positive cash flow outlook. The dividend will be paid on July 10, 2026 to shareholders of record as of June 30, 2026.

HIMX.headline: Himax Technologies, Inc. Declares Cash Dividend for FY2025
HIMX.description: Himax Technologies declared a cash dividend of 25.2 cents per ADS (12.6 cents per ordinary share) for 2025, representing a 100% payout ratio of last year's profit. The dividend will be paid on July 10, 2026 to shareholders of record as of June 30, 2026. CEO Jordan Wu highlighted the company's healthy balance sheet, positive cash flow outlook, and in-house manufacturing capacity in WLO and LCoS segments to support anticipated growth.

FTNT.headline: Nasdaq 100 Climbs Records, Oil Extends Drop As Iran Weighs Hormuz Deal: Stock Market Today
FTNT.description: U.S. tech stocks reached record highs on May 7, 2026, as crude oil tumbled nearly 3% on hopes Iran will accept a U.S. memorandum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Nasdaq 100 gained 0.4% to 28,706 while the S&P 500 rose 0.2% to 7,376. Earnings drove significant stock-specific moves, with major winners including AAON (+36%), Datadog (+30%), and H&R Block (+26%), while notable losers included Planet Fitness (-33%), Insmed (-22%), and Zoetis (-22%).

FTNT.headline: Stock Market Today: Do, S&P 500, Nasdaq Futures Gain As Trump Celebrates Record Gains— Fortinet, Whirlpool, Core Scientific In Focus (UPDATED)
FTNT.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday following Wednesday's record rally, with President Trump celebrating all-time high stock market gains and strong job growth. Key movers included Fortinet gaining 12.76% on strong earnings, while Beyond Meat and Whirlpool declined significantly. BlackRock maintains a pro-risk stance on U.S. equities, citing strong corporate earnings momentum and AI revenue growth.

WOLF.headline: This Wall Street Analyst Has a Simple Method for Finding the Next Chip Stock Winners. Will It Pay Off?
WOLF.description: Citrini Research suggests finding winning semiconductor stocks by identifying laggards that haven't surged yet and still trade at lower valuations. The theory focuses on Edge AI as the next breakthrough, with companies like Qualcomm, Skyworks, and Wolfspeed positioned to benefit as AI expands into consumer devices and applications beyond data centers.

RIOT.headline: Stock Market Today, May 5: Cipher Digital Surges on AI Data Center Shift Backed by Hyperscale Leases
RIOT.description: Cipher Mining (CIFR) surged 23.53% on May 5, 2026, despite weak Q1 results, driven by a strategic pivot toward AI and high-performance computing data centers. The company secured a new $200 million credit facility and announced approximately $11.4 billion in contracted revenue, signaling a shift from bitcoin mining to data center infrastructure.

DVA.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
DVA.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

FLEX.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
FLEX.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

POET.headline: Should You Buy the Dip in Poet Technologies Stock?
POET.description: Poet Technologies, a photonic solutions specialist for AI data centers, has seen its stock plummet 70% from all-time highs following a confidentiality dispute over a customer order from Marvell Technology's subsidiary. While the company's optical interconnect technology shows promise for addressing AI infrastructure challenges, Poet remains unprofitable with minimal revenue and significant execution risks, making it a highly speculative investment suitable only for risk-tolerant investors.

POET.headline: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges POET Technologies Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
POET.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against POET Technologies Inc. alleging securities fraud. The complaint claims the company misrepresented its tax status as a potential passive foreign investment company (PFIC), failed to disclose negative tax implications for U.S. shareholders, and that an officer violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased POET securities between April 1-27, 2026.

AUR.headline: How a Berkshire Hathaway Company Helped Aurora Innovation Stock Soar Today
AUR.description: Aurora Innovation announced a partnership with Berkshire Hathaway's McClane Company to expand autonomous delivery services in Texas, building on a successful pilot program that delivered 1,400 loads with 100% on-time delivery. The company also expanded its self-driving program with Volvo Group, launching a new 200-mile route between Dallas and Oklahoma City. Aurora's stock surged 8.73% on the news.

SMCI.headline: DEADLINE ALERT for ATRA, COTY, SMCI, IBRX: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith Reminds Investors of Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Class Actions
SMCI.description: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith has announced securities fraud class action lawsuits against four publicly-traded companies: Atara Biotherapeutics, Coty Inc., Super Micro Computer, and ImmunityBio. The lawsuits allege that defendants made false or misleading statements regarding their business operations and prospects. Investors have until late May 2026 to file lead plaintiff motions.

SMCI.headline: Super Micro Surges Over 20% as Margins Soar, Sales Fall Short
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer surged over 24% post-earnings despite missing revenue guidance by over $2 billion ($10.24B vs. $12.3B expected). The company beat adjusted EPS estimates significantly (84 cents vs. 63 cents expected) due to a dramatic 370 basis point improvement in gross margin to 10.1%. However, the margin improvement came as a result of lower sales, with guidance suggesting margins will decline to 8.3% as revenue recovers. The stock faces ongoing uncertainty from the indictment of co-found

SMCI.headline: Super Micro Computer Shareholder Alert: ClaimsFiler Reminds Investors With Losses In Excess Of $100,000 Of Lead Plaintiff Deadline In Class Action Lawsuits Against Super Micro Computer, Inc. - SMCI
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer faces securities class action lawsuits after three company executives were indicted for allegedly diverting approximately $2.5 billion worth of AI servers to China in violation of U.S. export control laws between 2024-2025. The company failed to disclose material information during the class period (February 2, 2024 - March 19, 2026). The stock price fell 33.3% to $20.53 on March 20, 2026 following the DOJ announcement. Investors with losses exceeding $100,000 have until May

SMCI.headline: Super Micro Computer Ignites AI Rally With Strong Sales and Earnings Guidance
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) surged 22.44% after reporting quarterly sales of $10.24 billion (122.6% YoY growth) and providing forward guidance of $11.75 billion in sales and 72 cents EPS, both exceeding analyst expectations. The company's operating margins expanded to 6.1% from 3.2% YoY. Memory-related stocks like Micron Technology and Seagate Technology also rallied amid the AI surge. The positive ADP payroll report (109,000 jobs created in April) supports expectations for strong employment dat

SMCI.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
SMCI.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

SMCI.headline: Why Super Micro Computer Stock Soared Today
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer stock surged 16.6% after beating earnings expectations with $0.84 per share versus forecasted $0.62, despite missing on sales ($10.2B vs. $12.4B expected). However, the company's free cash flow has dramatically deteriorated to negative $7.7 billion year-to-date from positive $690 million last year, raising concerns about sustainability despite strong AI server demand and projected $38.9-40.4 billion in annual sales.

SMCI.headline: AI Earnings Reaction: AMD Leads, SMCI Survives, Nasdaq Now Must Prove It
SMCI.description: AMD delivered strong AI earnings with a 16%+ overnight rally, driven by data center growth and EPYC/Instinct products. Super Micro Computer (SMCI) rallied 15% on forward guidance despite missing revenue. Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) remained range-bound. The Nasdaq-100 faces a critical test: whether the gap-up from positive AI earnings can hold or if the move represents exhaustion and profit-taking.

SMCI.headline: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Rise As Trump Pauses 'Project Freedom'— Super Micro Computer, AMD, Intel In Focus  (UPDATED)
SMCI.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Wednesday following President Trump's announcement of a temporary pause in 'Project Freedom,' a military operation escorting commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones advanced 0.59% and 0.66% respectively. Tech stocks led gains, with Super Micro Computer jumping 17.21% on strong earnings, AMD surging 18.64% on better-than-expected results, and Intel advancing 6.40% after clearing an antitrust hurdle. The Fed is expected to hold rates stea

SMCI.headline: ChronoScale Appoints Cenly Chen as Chief Executive Officer to Lead Next Phase of AI Compute Growth
SMCI.description: ChronoScale Corporation announced the appointment of Cenly Chen as Chief Executive Officer, bringing over two decades of enterprise infrastructure and high-performance computing experience from Super Micro Computer. The appointment comes as demand for AI compute infrastructure accelerates, with ChronoScale positioning itself to expand capacity and deliver consistent performance at scale.

SMCI.headline: Why Is Super Micro Stock Soaring Wednesday?
SMCI.description: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) reported mixed Q3 results with EPS beating expectations at 84 cents vs. 62 cents consensus, but revenue missing at $10.24B vs. $12.33B expected. The company is experiencing strong AI infrastructure growth with over 80% of revenue from AI GPU platforms. SMCI issued upbeat Q4 guidance with EPS of 65-79 cents and revenue of $11-12.5B, driving shares up 17.32% in premarket trading.

AMD.headline: Here's Why This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Just Exploded Past Wall Street's Most Bullish Price Target
AMD.description: DigitalOcean's stock surged 40% after reporting strong Q1 2026 results, with AI-related revenue jumping 221% year-over-year. The cloud computing company is aggressively building AI infrastructure for small and medium-sized businesses through its new AI-Native Cloud platform. While the stock has exceeded analyst price targets, it now trades at a premium valuation, requiring investors to maintain a long-term outlook of at least three years.

AMD.headline: AMD Stock Gets A Racehorse-Sized Bull Call From Jim Cramer
AMD.description: Jim Cramer compared AMD to Secretariat, the legendary racehorse, suggesting the company could become a runaway winner in the AI chip market. Cramer defended AMD after a recent downgrade, arguing that AI-driven demand and CEO Lisa Su's execution could help the company close the gap with Nvidia and capture greater market share. AMD stock was down 3.51% at the time of publication but has gained 86.3% over the past month.

AMD.headline: 8 Stocks Yet to Join the Rally With Upside Potential of Up to 85%
AMD.description: Following a market rally driven by US-Iran peace deal hopes and strong tech earnings, eight underperforming US stocks are identified as having significant upside potential of 25-85%. While the S&P 500 and NASDAQ reached record highs, certain stocks have fallen 10-20% over two weeks despite strong fundamentals. IQVIA and Sonoco are highlighted as examples of beaten-down stocks trading well below fair value estimates with analyst upside targets of 20-97%.

AMD.headline: AI Stocks Were Just Put to the Test. Here's How They Fared.
AMD.description: AI stocks faced significant headwinds in early 2026 due to valuation concerns, with the S&P 500 Shiller CAPE ratio reaching 40 (matching dot-com bubble levels), and geopolitical uncertainty from the Iran war. However, AI stocks have rebounded strongly as tech giants demonstrated that their massive AI infrastructure spending is justified by solid customer commitments and strong revenue growth. Companies like Amazon are monetizing their $200 billion AI investments through committed customer deals,

AMD.headline: Narrow Tech Leadership Continues as Oil and Rates Fall
AMD.description: Technology sector significantly outperforms the broader market by 12.5 percentage points over the past 30 days, driven by major names like Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Broadcom, Micron, and AMD. However, this narrow market leadership concentrated in large-cap tech stocks poses correction risks. Oil prices fell sharply on U.S.-Iran deal hopes, while interest rates declined as the 10-year yield fell below its multi-year downtrend. AMD surged 18.61% after strong earnings, while ARM and Palantir showed

AMD.headline: This "Magnificent Seven" Stock Is the Cheapest of Them All. Is It a Buy Right Now?
AMD.description: Meta Platforms is trading at the lowest valuation among the Magnificent Seven stocks at 19.8x forward P/E ratio. Despite a 6% decline this year, the article argues the stock is undervalued given strong Q1 advertising revenue growth (33% YoY) and strategic AI investments. Investor skepticism centers on Meta's increased capital expenditure plans ($125-145 billion), though the author contends this spending is necessary to remain competitive in AI infrastructure.

AMD.headline: This Wall Street Analyst Has a Simple Method for Finding the Next Chip Stock Winners. Will It Pay Off?
AMD.description: Citrini Research suggests finding winning semiconductor stocks by identifying laggards that haven't surged yet and still trade at lower valuations. The theory focuses on Edge AI as the next breakthrough, with companies like Qualcomm, Skyworks, and Wolfspeed positioned to benefit as AI expands into consumer devices and applications beyond data centers.

AMD.headline: Michael Burry's Chip Short Just Got Wilder: SOXX Went From 43% Above Trend To 60%
AMD.description: Michael Burry's short position on the semiconductor sector ETF SOXX has become increasingly challenged as the fund rallied to $502, now trading 60% above its 200-day moving average—the highest gap since the ETF's inception in 2001. Burry purchased January 2027 puts at $330 strike when SOXX was at $455, requiring a 35% drawdown to break even. The extreme valuation stretch, combined with nine constituents trading even further above their trend lines, suggests either a sideways consolidation or sha

AMD.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 Surge: AMD Earnings Steal the Show at Midday
AMD.description: Stock markets rallied on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 up 1.3% and Nasdaq-100 up 1.7%, driven by AMD's blowout Q1 earnings report showing strong AI accelerator demand. AMD stock surged 17.5%, boosting the broader semiconductor sector including Nvidia (+5.3%). Oil prices dropped over 6% on reports of U.S.-Iran peace negotiations, though President Trump tempered deal expectations.

AMD.headline: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Smash Records As AMD Jumps 16% On AI Hype: Stock Market Today
AMD.description: U.S. equities hit fresh record highs on May 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reaching all-time peaks. AMD and Super Micro Computer surged 16% on strong AI-driven earnings, while oil prices collapsed 6-7% on diplomatic hopes between Washington and Tehran. Technology stocks led gains, though energy stocks declined sharply. Mixed earnings results saw winners like Flex Ltd. (+30%) and DaVita (+18%), while CDW and Coupang fell 20% and 17.5% respectively.

JOBY.headline: Why Did Joby Aviation Stock Fly Today?
JOBY.description: Joby Aviation stock surged 17.2% after reporting Q1 2026 results, highlighting operational achievements including successful eVTOL flight demonstrations and expected commercial operations later in 2026. However, the company remains pre-revenue and analysts project cash burn could deplete its $2.5 billion reserves by 2029, two years before achieving profitability.
